title: GitHub Projects v2 spec
description: Declarative spec for a GitHub Project (Projects v2). The `fields` half is applied by github/projects/bootstrap.sh; the optional `filed_by_field`/`sources` half configures the #73 hourly sync workflow (sync-project.sh). One file per Project under github/projects/. The format mixes desired state with continuity hints (option IDs) and two tools' concerns — see issue #90 for the cleaner split planned in a future tool.
$comment:
Enum values and the option-input shape mirror GitHub's GraphQL schema (which is the canonical source — GitHub does not publish a JSON Schema for Projects v2). To refresh against introspection, run:

  gh api graphql -f query='{ color: __type(name: "ProjectV2SingleSelectFieldOptionColor") { enumValues { name } } fieldType: __type(name: "ProjectV2CustomFieldType") { enumValues { name } } optionInput: __type(name: "ProjectV2SingleSelectFieldOptionInput") { inputFields { name } } }'

ProjectV2CustomFieldType also includes ITERATION; the script does not yet provision iteration fields, so the type enums below intentionally omit it.

owner.description: GitHub login that owns the project (user or organisation). Standard 1–39-char login rule: alphanumerics with single internal hyphens; no leading, trailing, or consecutive hyphens.

title.description: Project title. Lookup is by title, not number, so renaming the Project in the UI requires updating this spec.

fields.description: Custom fields the script manages. Built-in fields (Title, Assignees, …) and any field not listed here are left alone.
fields.$comment: GitHub Projects v2 documents a 50-field-per-project cap that includes built-ins (~13). Custom-field budget is therefore around 37; this is a generous outer bound rather than a precise budget.

filed_by_field.description: Name of the TEXT field the #73 sync workflow stamps with each item's source. Consumed by sync-project.sh, not by bootstrap.sh; should name a field declared in `fields`.

status_field.description: Name of the SINGLE_SELECT field the #73 sync workflow sets per item type. Consumed by sync-project.sh, not by bootstrap.sh; should name a field declared in `fields`. Requires `pr_status` and `issue_status`.

pr_status.description: Option name in `status_field` assigned to pull requests. Consumed by sync-project.sh; must match an option declared on that field.

issue_status.description: Option name in `status_field` assigned to issues. Consumed by sync-project.sh; must match an option declared on that field.

sources.description: Issue/PR sources the #73 sync workflow mirrors into the board. Consumed by sync-project.sh, not by bootstrap.sh.

source.description: One issue/PR source for the #73 sync workflow. `owner` sources use `logins` (accounts whose repos are scanned); `author`/`assignee` use a single `login`.

type.description: Subset of GraphQL ProjectV2CustomFieldType. The script splits SINGLE_SELECT off into its own field variant because it has options + colors to converge.
Subset of GraphQL ProjectV2CustomFieldType minus SINGLE_SELECT (handled separately) and minus ITERATION (not yet supported by the script).

option.description: Mirrors GraphQL ProjectV2SingleSelectFieldOptionInput (id, name, color, description).

id.description: GitHub-assigned option ID. Not declarative — it's a continuity hint so a UI rename can be reverted to the canonical name without orphaning items (matching is ID-first, name second). Capture the ID after creation; omit for new options. Tracked separately in the cleaner tool planned in #90.
